Tests the cosine function of PI using the `with` statement and asserts the result is -1.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of the `with` statement to access properties of the `Math` object. It declares two variables, `x` and `y`, and then uses the `with` statement to set `x` to the cosine of `PI`, which is a property of the `Math` object. The script then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the value of `x` is equal to `-1`, which is the expected result of `cos(PI)`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating the use of the `with` statement and the `Math` object in JavaScript.
